title: cassytha racemosa f. racemosa
intro: Cassytha racemosa f. racemosa, also known as the devil's twine, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Cassytha racemosa f. racemosa can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for herbal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically validated.
